mnemonics.title: Remember Traversal Orders
mnemonics.content: L ROOT R = INORDER (LEFT is first), ROOT L R = PREORDER (ROOT first), LR ROOT = POSTORDER (ROOT last). THINK: IN means ROOT IN THE MIDDLE.

mnemonics.title: Level-order BFS
mnemonics.content: QUEUE-based: FIRST IN FIRST OUT = LEVEL BY LEVEL. LIKE CHECKOUT LINE.

mnemonics.title: BST Sorted Output
mnemonics.content: INORDER GIVES SORT. IN for SORTED = INORDER.

mnemonics.title: Delete Tree Postorder
mnemonics.content: CHILDREN FIRST, THEN PARENT. POST = AFTER = CHILDREN AFTER, PARENT AFTER CHILDREN.

mnemonics.title: Expression Trees
mnemonics.content: POSTFIX = POST = evaluate AFTER operands. PREFIX = PRE = operator BEFORE operands.

acronyms.expansion: Depth-First Search - Traversals that go deep before wide (Inorder, Preorder, Postorder)
acronyms.usage: Used to describe tree traversals that explore depth before breadth

acronyms.expansion: Breadth-First Search - Traverses level by level, also called Level-order
acronyms.usage: Used for Level-order traversal using queue

acronyms.expansion: Left-Visitor-Right - Another name for Inorder traversal
Visitor-Left-Right - Another name for Preorder traversal
Left-Right-Visitor - Another name for Postorder traversal

visualTips.visualization: Draw a vertical line from each node. Inorder = nodes you hit when walking from left to right. Root sits in middle between left and right subtrees.
Think of taking a photo of each node BEFORE visiting its children. Root is captured first, then children.
Think of cleaning a room: children (subtrees) first, then the parent. Root is processed last.
Imagine water flowing from root, spreading to next level like ripples. Visit nodes in wavefront order.
Count edges on longest path from root to leaf. Single node = height 0. Root at level 1.

analogies.analogy: Inorder = clean left room, then living room, then right room. Preorder = clean living room first, then children rooms. Postorder = let kids clean their rooms before you clean the living room.
Preorder = read ancestor first, then descendants. Inorder = read family in chronological left-to-right order. Postorder = read all children before parents.
Going down is like visiting left subtree (recursion). Coming back up processes the node. For skewed tree, it's like climbing a tall ladder one rung at a time.
People enter (queue) from front door, sit in rows. First row (level 1) fills completely before second row. Like BFS queue processing.
